incorrects.description: Branch 2: Student selected valid answers (35 and/or 40 fit the pattern) but did not select both 13 and 42

steps.text: You found some answers that fit the 5s pattern. But the question asks which ones DON'T fit. Look at the endings again. Which numbers can't be multiples of 5?

incorrects.description: Branch 3: Student selected only incorrect answers (35 and/or 40, which DO fit the pattern)

params.description: All products on the 5s strip highlight.

steps.text: Multiples of 5 always end in 0 or 5. That's the pattern. The question asks which answers DON'T fit that rule. Look at what each number ends with.

incorrects.description: Branch 4: Student selected a mix of correct (13 and/or 42) and incorrect answers (35 and/or 40)

params.description: 5s strip updates to show pattern emphasis on endings.

steps.text: You found numbers that don't fit the 5s pattern. But you also selected some that do fit. Check the endings: 35 and 40 both end in 0 or 5, so they could be multiples of 5.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: 5s strip animates to show all products ending in 0 or 5. Then 13 and 42 pulse with a cross-out to show they don't fit.

steps.text: Let me show you. Every multiple of 5 ends in 0 or 5. Look at the strip: 5, 10, 15, 20, all the way to 50. Now check the choices. 13 ends in 3. 42 ends in 2. Neither fits the pattern. The answer is 13 and 42. Those are the ones you can eliminate.
